evidence: The paper discusses random forests in Section 2.3 as "an aggregation of a (typically large) number of classification or regression trees" for ensemble prediction and mentions conditional inference trees "avoid selection bias in cases where the covariates have different scales" citing Strobl et al. The literature review mentions only one published study using random forest (Fosbol et al., 2020 as sensitivity analysis). The paper's own examples use linear regression, best subset selection, boosting, and Bayesian methods, not random forest variable importance.
